results.title: Comparing AWS, Azure, GCP | DigitalOcean
results.content: Linux-based virtual machines, managed databases, managed Kubernetes, a Platform as a Service solution, a serverless/cloud functions solution, storage, and much more. Here are some reasons you may want to consider DigitalOcean as an alternative to Amazon Web Services, Google Cloud, and Microsoft Azure: Simple yet comprehensive product suite: DigitalOcean doesnât offer the hundreds of products found in AWS, Azure, and GCP, but does offer the core products that most businesses need, including [...] need, including VPS hosting, managed databases, managed Kubernetes, and more. ### Try DigitalOcean for free ## Related Resources ### Azure App Service vs DigitalOcean App Platform ### 13 Google Cloud Functions Alternatives to Improve Your Serverless Architecture ### AWS Hidden Costs: Identifying and Avoiding Them ## Get started for free Sign up and get $200 in credit for your first 60 days with DigitalOcean.\ \This promotional offer applies to new accounts only. [...] GCP offers a wide range of services, similar to AWS and Azure, however has fewer data centers than AWS and Azure and has solutions that are considered friendly for DevOps. Looking for an alternative to the big three cloud providers? ## Difference between AWS vs Azure vs Google Cloud AWS, Azure, and GCP provide comparable computing resources, including virtual machines and serverless computing options. results.title: Comparing AWS, Azure, and GCP for Startups in 2026 | DigitalOcean
results.content: communicate over a private IP space without the performance overhead of complex peering or VPNs. For AI-specific workloads, GCP offers custom TPUs designed specifically to accelerate large-scale machine learning training and inference. By integrating a foundational general-purpose cloud with Gradient™ AI, DigitalOcean provides the infrastructure and workflows required to build full-stack AI applications and agents without the operational overhead and cost complexity often found in hyperscale [...] AWS’s massive scale to Azure’s enterprise Microsoft integration to GCP’s data analytics leadership. The major hyperscalers differ in their strategic focus: AWS provides the most granular infrastructure control and AI model flexibility, Azure offers deep integration with the Microsoft ecosystem, and GCP excels in big data analytics and container-native networking. DigitalOcean provides a streamlined alternative to hyperscalers by offering an integrated agentic inference cloud architecture and [...] found in hyperscale environments. Integrated agentic cloud architecture: DigitalOcean provides a suite of approximately 30 core products, avoiding the complexity of the hundreds of specialized services found on AWS or Azure.
